title: Liabilities Project (Los Angeles City Attorney's Office)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Liabilities Project (Los Angeles City Attorney's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

- Copies of all reports, lists, databases, or individual documents created since 1/1/16 detail lawsuits brought against the city's police department. This should include any documentation mechanism your department uses to track lawsuits. It should also include any documents your department submits to local, state, or federal agencies listing the number and status of lawsuits brought against the police department. This request should include documents compiled quarterly, annually, or in other increments and documents or databases that contain responsive information over a longer period. 

- The records should include, if it exists, the following information: 
Settlement amounts
Case category (civil rights, collisions, etc) 
Case type/description (harassment, crowd control, in-custody death, etc)
Involved officers
Case numbers
Total expenses (this should include the legal costs to the city, including outsite litigation costs.) 

- Any corrective action reports that describe the incident that caused the lawsuit and the corrective actions taken to minimize risk, prevent recurrence, and hold the department and the involved officers accountable. The reports are often sent to respective bodies to approve settlements (city council, board of supervisors, controller, or risk management office) or created by another office within the city or county. 
- Copies of settlement agreements 

Only include settlements above $75,000 and lawsuits related to civil rights violations, collisions, professional negligence, and labor.

Search January 1, 2016 to today's date.

When possible, produce documents in spreadsheet format (excel, csv). California public records law requires the custodian of records to assist in finding records.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 days.

Sincerely,

J.

title: Sunshine Law Request - Missouri State Generative AI Records

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Sunshine Law Request - Missouri State Generative AI Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am writing on behalf of a team of researchers and journalists from Stanford University, the University of Southern California, KNKX Public Radio and the New York Times. We are studying how government agencies are adopting and using AI tools in their daily work.

I request records related to Missouri state government use of generative AI tools overseen by the Office of Administration, including internal chatbot prototypes and government-secured AI models, as described in Executive Order 26-02 (Jan 2026).

We are requesting the following records:
1. All interaction logs, prompts, inputs, outputs, conversation histories, and similar records created by employees using Google Gemini, Azure OpenAI or other models hosted in Missouri's secured tenant, internal chatbot prototypes, and any other generative AI chatbot tools used by Missouri state agencies for the period from April 7, 2025 through the date this request is processed. Please provide these records in their native electronic format where available, including HTML exports, JSON, CSV, ZIP exports, or equivalent structured formats.
2. Any administrative usage logs, dashboards, reports, or other records sufficient to show which employees, offices, or units had access to these tools and the volume of usage during the same period.
3. Any policies, guidelines, training materials, approval documents, acceptable-use rules, or governance materials governing employee use of these tools or any other generative AI chatbot tools.
4. Any records retention, deletion, preservation, or disposition policies applicable to these interaction logs or related AI-generated records.

To facilitate your response, we note that ChatGPT conversation histories can be exported by individual users via Settings > Data Controls > Export Data, which generates a downloadable ZIP file containing conversations in HTML format. For Claude, export is similar, except via “Settings > Privacy > Export Data”. For Microsoft Copilot, interaction histories may be available through the Microsoft 365 admin console, the Purview compliance center, or individual user account settings. We are happy to provide additional technical guidance if that would be helpful.

We are seeking the substantive content of AI interactions for research purposes. We do not need personally identifying information of individual employees (such as names and email addresses).

We note that records of this type have been made available in response to public records requests in other jurisdictions. See Cascade PBS, WA City Officials Are Using ChatGPT to Write Government Documents (Aug. 2025), https://www.cascadepbs.org/news/2025/08/wa-city-officials-are-using-chatgpt-to-write-government-documents/ (reporting on AI-generated government documents obtained through public records requests in Everett, WA); see also Texas State AI Usage — Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT), MuckRock, https://www.muckrock.com/foi/texas-109/texas-state-ai-usage-texas-department-of-transportation-txdot-196227/#files (collecting records of AI usage at the Texas Department of Transportation released in response to a public records request).

The results of this research will be published in peer-reviewed academic venues and will contribute to public understanding of how public servants interact with AI systems. This request is made by an academic research institution for scholarly, non-commercial purposes; as such, we request a fee waiver or reduction. If a fee waiver is not granted, please inform me of estimated costs before proceeding. I am willing to pay fees up to $20.

If you contend that any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please identify the specific legal basis for the withholding and release all reasonably segregable non-exempt material. If any part of this request is unclear, I am happy to discuss ways to clarify or narrow it while preserving the core records sought.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jacqueline Xiong

title: CPRA Request: Research Funding, DSP Operations, and Employment Records (UC Berkeley, 2022–2026) (Office Of The President (ucop), University Of California)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CPRA Request: Research Funding, DSP Operations, and Employment Records (UC Berkeley, 2022–2026) (Office Of The President (ucop), University Of California)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I request records reflecting research funding received by Professor Sheri L. Johnson at UC Berkeley from 2023 to 2026, including:

Total amounts and funding sources
Grant agreements or award documents
Budget summaries and expenditure categories (e.g., personnel, equipment, travel, overhead)
Any reports or documents reflecting allocation or use of such funds

I am not requesting personally identifiable information; aggregated or redacted records are sufficient.

I also request aggregated records (2023–2026) regarding the Disabled Students’ Program (DSP), including:

Total funding received (including government sources) and annual budgets
The number of accommodation requests, approvals, denials, and fulfillment rates
The number of complaints alleging failure to provide accommodations, including outcome categories
Aggregated, de-identified demographic breakdowns (e.g., age ranges, nationality categories), where available

I am not requesting personally identifiable student information; aggregated or redacted data will suffice.

I further request records related to DSP operations during the 2022–2023 labor action involving DSP advisors, including:

Communications, reports, or policy documents regarding service disruptions
Aggregated or de-identified complaint summaries related to DSP accommodation failures during this period

Additionally, I request employment-related records for Marilyn de la Cruz, including:

Employment contracts or offer letters
Job descriptions and role responsibilities
Salary ranges or compensation structure, where available

I am not requesting personally identifiable student information or confidential personnel evaluations; redacted or aggregated records will suffice.

Lastly,
I request records of communications between UCOP and UC Berkeley regarding:

The processing and handling of my CPRA requests
Administrative coordination related to my Government Claim and demand letter

This request excludes attorney-client privileged communications; however, I request that any non-privileged portions be disclosed, with redactions applied where necessary.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Young Jin Jung

communications.subject: Jung
communications.communication:
Dear Sir/Madam,
The only records identified at UCOP that are responsive to your recent CA Public Records Act (CPRA) request are attached.

Please note that all the other records you requested would be held at UCB. You will need to submit your request directly to the campus CPRA office here: pra@berekely.edu<mailto:pra@berekely.edu>

Sincerely,
UCOP CPRA Office

title: GO-Biz: Atomic Machines, Inc. - California Competes Tax Credit (CCTC) Agreement Documentation

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: GO-Biz: Atomic Machines, Inc. - California Competes Tax Credit (CCTC) Agreement Documentation
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I hereby request the following records related to the California Competes Tax Credit (CCTC) Agreement between GO-Biz and Atomic Machines, Inc. (https://www.atomicmachines.com). This agreement was widely reported and can be found online here: https://business.ca.gov/wp-content/uploads/Atomic-Machines-Inc.-CCTC-Agreement.pdf

Agreement dated approximately November 2025):

Compliance Reports: All annual compliance reports, worksheets, and supporting documentation submitted by Atomic Machines, Inc. for Taxable Year 2025 (and 2026 to date) to demonstrate the achievement of milestones related to net increase of full-time employees and investment amounts.

Milestone Evaluations: Any written determinations or correspondence from GO-Biz verifying that Atomic Machines, Inc. has met its "Project/Milestone" requirements as set forth in Section 3 and Exhibit A of the Agreement.

Recapture or Breach Notices: Any records, notices, or internal memoranda discussing potential "Events of Breach" (pursuant to Section 10 of the Agreement) or the "Recapture" of tax credits due to the company’s failure to maintain hiring or investment targets.

Wage Verifications: Redacted payroll summaries or certifications provided by the company to verify it has met the "Minimum Annual Wage" and "Cumulative Average Annual Wage" commitments for California full-time employees.

Relocation Disclosures: Any communications from the company regarding its certification that, absent this credit, it would have "terminated all or a portion of its employees in California or relocate[d]... to another state.

This request is made in the public interest to ensure transparency regarding the allocation of $15,000,000 in taxpayer-funded credits - especially considering the reputation of Atomic Machine's leadership.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robert Delaware

title: Flock Safety Records

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Flock Safety Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

1) Invoices, Contracts, and Purchase orders related to the company Flock Safety or Flock Group (Flock).

2) I request internal training documents and policies and procedures for the use of Flock tools.

3) I request any sharing agreements signed with other agencies as well as The City's sharing agreement it requires other agencies to conform to.

4) I request the web address of The City's Flock transparency portal, a free feature of Flock OS.

5) Lastly, I request all available audit data from the Flock OS audit tools. This should include the organization audit, network audit, and sharing report.
    A. Organization Audit in Flock.
    The report should include data logged from the period of installation to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done by the City.

    B. Network Audit in Flock.
    The report should include data logged from the period of installation, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the City’s Flock network by any agency the City has shared with in the Flock System.

    C. Sharing report in Flock.
    The report should include flock networks shared with The City and networks The City is sharing its network with.

I request that files be supplied in their original electronic, machine readable format rather than being converted to any alternate formats.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael

title: Federal government Rule 11 letters (Department of Justice, Civil Rights Division)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Federal government Rule 11 letters (Department of Justice, Civil Rights Division)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. In any litigation before a federal district court on behalf of the United States or any agency, department, official, or employee thereof, any letter sent by your attorneys containing any of the following terms: 
a. "Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 11"
b. "Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, Rule 11"
c. "FRCP 11"
d. "Fed. R. Civ. P. 11"
e. "Fed. R. Civ. P., Rule 11"

2. Any policies, manuals, or guidance (not including guidance specific to a particular case) concerning pursuit of Rule 11 sanctions.

This request is limited to correspondence sent (or policies, manuals, or guidance utilized) on or after January 1, 2020.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Adam Steinbaugh

communications.subject: RE: [EXTERNAL] Freedom of Information Act Request: Federal government Rule 11 letters (Department of Justice, Civil Rights Division)
communications.communication:
Mr. Steinbaugh,


Thank you for contacting the Civil Rights Division Freedom of Information Act Unit. The Civil Rights Division is in receipt of your below FOIA request for any litigation before a federal district court on behalf of the United States or any agency, department, official, or employee thereof, any letter sent by your attorneys containing any of the following terms: "Federal Rule of Civil Procedure 11"; "Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, Rule 11"; "FRCP 11"; "Fed. R. Civ. P. 11"; "Fed. R. Civ. P., Rule 11".
The number of potential custodians is too expansive to conduct a search of approximately 700 career employees.  Therefore, if you want federal records from the Civil Rights Division, please narrow your request to target specific custodians or Section within the Civil Rights Division you would like a search to be conducted (i.e., political appointees, AAG, Disability Rights, Criminal, Special Litigation, Employment Litigation, Housing and Civil Enforcement).

Upon receipt of this additional information, we will search our files for information responsive to your request

Thank You,

FOI/PA Unit
U.S. Department of Justice
Civil Rights Division
CRT.FOIArequests@usdoj.gov<mailto:CRT.FOIArequests@usdoj.gov>

communications.communication:
ADB
Ack Ltr

U.S. Department of Justice
Civil Rights Division
Freedom of Information/PA Unit -4CON
950 Pennsylvania Ave., NW
Washington, DC 20530


Via Electronic Mail Only
183748-10164237@requests.muckrock.com<mailto:183748-10164237@requests.muckrock.com>


Date Received: December 1, 2026

FOI/PA No. CASENUMBER          26-00043-F


Dear Mr. Steinbaugh:

The Civil Rights Division acknowledges receipt of your FOIA request, which was received in this office on the date indicated above.  Your request seeks access to records about cases opened since January 1, 2025 through November 1, 2025, where the case description or subject matter involves the First Amendment.  Please read this letter carefully because it contains important information about your request.

            We have initiated a search to locate records, if any, that are within the scope of your request.  We will follow up with you once searches have been completed and any responsive records subject to FOIA/Privacy Act have been processed to segregate non-exempt information for release.

            As a result of the large number of FOIA and Privacy Acts requests received by the Civil Rights Division, we may encounter delays in processing your request.  To ensure that requesters are treated fairly, the Division processes requests in the approximate order of receipt.  Please be assured that your request is being handled as equitably and promptly as possible.  As a result of the large number of FOIA and Privacy Acts requests received by the Civil Rights Division, we may encounter delays in processing your request.  To ensure that requesters are treated fairly, the Division processes requests in the approximate order of receipt.  Please be assured that your request is being handled as equitably and promptly as possible. If you have any questions regarding the status of your request, you may contact Andrea Banks at crt.foiacomms@usdoj.gov<mailto:crt.foiacomms@usdoj.gov>. Please use the subject line: FOIA Request 26-00043-F (ADB) and reference the FOIA/PA No. above in any communications with the Civil Rights Division about your request


Additionally, you may contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) at the National Archives and Records Administration to inquire about the FOIA mediation services they offer.  The contact information for OGIS is:  Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001; e-mail at ogis@nara.gov<mailto:ogis@nara.gov>; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or facsimile at 202-741-5769.  Please reference the FOIA/PA No. above in any correspondence with OGIS.

Finally, if you are not satisfied with the Civil Rights Division's determination in response to this request, you may administratively appeal by writing to the Director, Office of Information Policy (OIP), United States Department of Justice, 441 G Street, NW, 6th Floor, Washington, D.C. 20530, or you may submit an appeal through OIP's FOIA STAR portal by creating an account following the instructions on OIP's website: https://www.justice.gov/oip/submit-and-track-request-or-appeal. Your appeal must be postmarked or electronically transmitted within 90 days of the date of my response to your request. If you submit your appeal by mail, both the letter and the envelope should be clearly marked "Freedom of Information Act Appeal."  Please reference the FOIA/PA No. above in any correspondence with OIP.

Thank you for your interest in the Civil Rights Division.


Andrea D. Banks

Andrea Banks, GIS
FOI/PA Unit
Civil Rights Division
U.S. Department of Justice
950 Pennsylvania Ave. N.W.
4CON 6th Floor
(202) 514-4210

title: Performance Evaluation (Oyster River Cooperative School District)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Performance Evaluation (Oyster River Cooperative School District)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of the most recent performance evaluation for the current district superintendent at the time of this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

Executive Asst. to the Superintendents
Oyster River Cooperative School District
SAU #5
36 Coe Drive
Durham, NH 03824
603-389-3286

The Right-To-Know Law provides that most e-mail communication, to or from School District employees regarding the business of the School District, are governmental records available to the public upon request.  Therefore, this e-mail communication may be subject to public disclosure.  However, confidential information about students, staff, and parents may not be subject to disclosure.  The confidentiality of information about students and personnel matters must be maintained by the individual or entity to which this e-mail is addressed.  Any unauthorized review, use, disclosure, or distribution of confidential information is prohibited.

title: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (San Bernardino County Registrar of Voters)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CA CVR Data Request (2022, 2024) (San Bernardino County Registrar of Voters)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Cast vote record (CVR) data for the following elections:
1. 2022 Nov Midterm Election
2. 2024 Nov General Election

Specifically, for each of these elections, I’m looking for the full anonymized record of votes cast generated by the relevant voting system technology in San Bernardino County in that year. Alameda County has an example of these records posted for the 2024 General Election on their Election Information for November 2024 under the name “Cast Vote Records (CVR) Report”.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Aleksandra Conevska

communications.communication:
San Bernardino County ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #25-30627: Dear Aleksandra, A Cast Vote Record Report that contains ballot data only and is void of any voter information may be extracted and compiled. In order to provide you with the Cast Vote Record Reports (not ballot images) the County will need to extract and compile the data. It is estimated it will take ROV staff approximately one hour and fifteen minutes to generate the data for the November 2022 election and approximately one hour and fifteen minutes to generate the data for the November 2024 election. The fee for this process is $310.13 per hour. The total amount estimated to fulfill your request is $620.26. The records for the report you have requested for the November 2022 election are currently set for destruction in accordance with Elections Code section 17300 et seq. in approximately 10 days. In order to provide you with the Cast Vote Record Report for the November 2022 election please submit the fee of $310.13 within the next 10 days to the San Bernardino County Registrar of Voters at 777 E. Rialto Ave., San Bernardino, CA 92415. Once the Registrar of Voters has received your payment for either the November 2022 election, November 2024 election or both, the County will begin to extract and compile the information requested. If payment is not received within the allotted 10 days, the Cast Vote Record Report for the November 2022 election will be destroyed as scheduled. Sincerely, Thalia Amador Elections Specialist ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at San Bernardino County.

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request #25-30640
communications.communication:
Hi there,

Thank you for the timely and helpful information, and for giving me advanced notice regarding the timeline for the destruction of the November 2022 election records. I unfortunately was not able to get back to you in a timely manner, and so I understand the records are now permanently out of reach. Thank you for alerting me in advance that this may happen.

I'm writing now to followup about the November 2024 election records. I would like to proceed with my request for those records, assuming they are still available, and am happy to pay the associated fee you outlined in your original response. 

When you have a chance, could you please confirm how I can submit payment to the San Bernardino County Registrar of Voters for the fee of $310.13? Is it only possible to pay through mailed check? Or is there an alternative way to process the payment online? Please let me know what is best. 

Thank you again for your patience, and your most diligent assistance with my request(s).  

All my best,
Aleksandra

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request #25-30640
communications.communication:
Hi,

Thank you kindly for this information, and for your help with my request. I am trying to make the payment now but I called the number you wrote in our most recent correspondence to make payment (909-387-8300), and I was told that it was not the correct line for making payments. 

Could you please let me know if there is an alternative number I can use? It is much easier for me to make payment by phone than by mail. But if payment by phone is not possible, I understand and will mail a check as soon as possible. Just let me know. 

All best,
Aleksandra

title: Request for Court Records and Business Licsenses for Griffin Brown (City Of Reno)

communications.subject: Nevada Public Records Act Request: Request for Court Records and Business Licsenses for Griffin Brown (City Of Reno)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. All court records for Griffin Brown between 2011 and 2026 in the state of Nevada. 
2. All current business licenses held by Griffin Brown in Washoe County between 2011 and 2026.
3. All canceled or former business licenses previously held by Griffin Brown in Washoe County between 2011 and 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Madison Kitch

communications.subject: General Public Records :: G020071-040726
communications.communication:
Dear Madison : 
 
Thank you for your interest in public records of the City of Reno. 
Your request was received in this office on April 07, 2026 and given the reference number: #G020071-040726 for tracking purposes.  Request Details: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

1. All court records for Griffin Brown between 2011 and 2026 in the state of Nevada. 
2. All current business licenses held by Griffin Brown in Washoe County between 2011 and 2026. 
3. All canceled or former business licenses previously held by Griffin Brown in Washoe County between 2011 and 2026. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Madison Kitch 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2259946/ 
 
Your request will be forwarded to the appropriate City department(s) to locate the information requested. Pursuant to NRS 239.0107 we will notify you within five (5) business days of receipt of the anticipated date your records will be available, and any estimated cost to produce them. Please note any confidential, proprietary, or protected information will be redacted prior to the release of your records. 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. 
Again, thank you for using the City of Reno Public Records Center.

communications.subject: General Public Records :: G020071-040726
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

Dear Madison , 
RE: Public Records Request Reference # G020071-040726. 
The City of Reno received a public records request from you on April 07, 2026. Your request mentioned: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

1. All court records for Griffin Brown between 2011 and 2026 in the state of Nevada. 
2. All current business licenses held by Griffin Brown in Washoe County between 2011 and 2026. 
3. All canceled or former business licenses previously held by Griffin Brown in Washoe County between 2011 and 2026. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Madison Kitch 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2259946/ This records request requires information held by the courts. We have no responsive records to #1. . All court records for Griffin Brown between 2011 and 2026 in the state of Nevada. 
Regarding the other records, we will process the rest of your request for you. If you have any additional questions, please feel free to contact the City Clerk's Office Public Records Division at 775-348-3932. 
Thank you for contacting the City of Reno. 
Sincerely, 
City of Reno 
City Clerk's Office Please note any confidential, proprietary, or protected information will be redacted prior to the release of the records. There could be a charge for copies of public records. If the estimated cost of the copies requested is $25.00 or more, the requester will be required to pay in full prior to reproduction. Materials will be held for 14 days. If not retrieved, the requester will be charged in full for a second reproduction in addition to any unpaid original charges. Advance payment will be forfeited if material is not retrieved.

RE: General Public Records of April 07, 2026, Reference # G020071-040726 
Dear Madison Kitch, 
The City of Reno received a request from you on April 07, 2026. Your request mentioned: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

1. All court records for Griffin Brown between 2011 and 2026 in the state of Nevada. 
2. All current business licenses held by Griffin Brown in Washoe County between 2011 and 2026. 
3. All canceled or former business licenses previously held by Griffin Brown in Washoe County between 2011 and 2026. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Madison Kitch 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2259946/ 
The City of Reno has reviewed its files and has located responsive records to your request. Please log in to the City of Reno Public Records Center at the following link to retrieve the responsive records. General Public Records - G020071-040726 
This shall serve to close out your request. If you have any additional questions, please feel free to contact us by responding to this email. 
PLEASE NOTE: The Nevada Revised Statutes does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions about the records you receive. 
Sincerely, 
City of Reno

title: 2026 Brady List (Pacific County Prosecuting Attorney)

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: 2026 Brady List (Pacific County Prosecuting Attorney)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

title: FOIA - Michael A. Taylor Incident

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: FOIA - Michael A. Taylor Incident
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

An electronic copy of the bodycam footage regarding the Michael A. Taylor incident that occurred on October 22, 2024 after a traffic stop at the Dollar General located at 914 Sheridan Rd, Winthrop Harbor, IL 60096. Taylor was arrested on the charges of bribery, aggravated battery to a peace officer and resisting a peace officer causing injury.

Specifically, I am seeking to obtain electronic copies of the:

1. BWC footage recorded by Winthrop Harbor Police Department's responding officers (main officer and assisting) detailing their full involvement in this incident

To assist you in locating the footage here are the key details of the incident:
Date of Event: October 22, 2024
Names: Michael A. Taylor
Location of Event: 914 Sheridan Rd, Winthrop Harbor, IL 60096

Please let me know if you require further information to process it.

If any aspects of this request are denied, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and please notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Johnson

title: Bend Flock Records 2024-2026

communications.subject: Oregon Public Records Law Request: Bend Flock Records 2024-2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Oregon Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am looking for a copy of all public records previously produced from 2024 to the present and related to the use of Flock and any other Automated License Plate Readers (ALPRs) in the City, including cameras used by the Bend Police. Previously produced records may include maps, contracts, user agreements, audit logs, and other similar items. By requesting previously produced records, I am hopeful that this request will require minimal labor to locate, review and produce the records. Please let me know if you have any questions. This request is per ORS 192.001 to 192.990.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Southern Oregon Flock Off

communications.subject: City of Bend Police Department - Police Department Request PD-2026-1183: Completed
communications.communication:
RE: Request Number PD-2026-1183 submitted on 04/05/2026 
  
 Dear Southern Oregon Flock Off, 
  
 Attached please find the responsive records for your public records request. The City of Bend’s FLOCK cameras were installed beginning in early 2025 and fully discontinued as of January 8, 2026. The records attached were previously provided to another requestor and include the following:  
  
 1.    Flock Agreement 
  
 •    October 10, 2024, signed Agreement between the City of Bend and Flock Safety, an amendment dated July 21, 2025. 
  
 
2.    Training Materials 
  
 •    The City of Bend Police Department provided in-person training on the Flock program. The attached Flock Camera Basic Training document was referenced during the training sessions. 
  
 •    Additional training was completed via the Flock website: 
  
 https://url4102.request.justfoia.com/ls/click?upn=u001.nrfdmL-2B1UhWpB4V2ArB2sdEiOhV2sGCNd-2BqfzgTS7Y22w4RRDYgGbRd4GeF8CiI5VuCI_XQpzFalsfwP6vLLSanJsMEc2kpb-2F6Xz-2FaOVhzPohSMHtUdwF4WA6xsGs00JBz9L4e4QN9pXhWU9s4n-2FQsHDb-2BokPSyFzJiGxmTJ7T1FP1Cj6DEzw3XMrO1a-2BRIyRSnHk2HXQD0f9GirabPwk8zHtrW9GU56A1QgylAxUWR8kN6v68iV5DJnKcc3DhmMcj3G1zZYuIblIr0AQml9ioJLNh4GeauFii3sy4l0YmUWOznskvwlCA5M-2FM0DdqQhQx8KYhdymRpBlOthvSvSdrTpOSi-2B19DDB7DIZMCku54Ij6PcuEMUloTdJWC0-2BRn6A2VGe 
  
 
3.    Organization Audit of ALPR Searches (June 2025 - December 2025) 
  
 •    Included columns: Org Name, Total Networks Searched, Total Devices Searched, Time Frame, Reason, Case#, Filters, Search Time, and Search Type. 
  
 •    Information in the "Reason", "Case#", and "Filters" columns have been redacted in accordance with applicable ORS public records exemptions. 
  
 
4.    Network Audit of ALPR Searches (June 2025 - December 2025) 
  
 •    Included columns: Org Name, Total Networks Searched, Total Devices Searched, Time Frame, Reason, Case#, Filters, Search Time, and Search Type. 
  
 •    Information in the "Reason", "Case#", and "Filters" columns have been redacted in accordance with applicable ORS public records exemptions. 
  
 
5.    Records of Organizations with Shared ALPR Cameras 
  
 
6.    City of Bend's Flock Administrative Settings 
  
 •    Administrative settings within the FLOCK program for the Bend Police Department as of December 3, 2025. 
  
 
7.    Locations of where the ALPRs were located in Bend as of 12/2/2025. 
  
 

The request Request Number: PD-2026-1183 has been completed. Please click on the request link to download the response documentation. 
  
 

Thank you, 
  
 


City of Bend Police Department 
  
 555 NE 15th St 
  
 Bend, OR 97701 
  
 (541) 322-2960

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: Facial Recognition and Surveillance Scan
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

For the dates of 6/1/2025 through 3/10/2026
Please provide all emails between Minneapolis Police Department personnel and the following domains:
1) @flocksafety.com
2) @insightlpr.com

If there is a newsletter sent out to many different MPD employees, you may de-duplicate to minimize responsive documents (we are only looking for unique examples of each email so feel free to de-duplicate if it is easier for you). Thank you!

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

Contact us 
 This resolved request will remain under My Open Requests on the OpenCity Portal for two weeks. During that time, you may contact us regarding your request through the portal, or by replying to this email.

title: LIHTC & MTBA Documents

communications.subject: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: LIHTC & MTBA Documents
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Tennessee Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

(1) A log or table of the LIHTC 9% competitive submissions/applications for 2018 (equivalent to the posting that exists for other years like 2020 at https://s3.amazonaws.com/thda.org/Documents/Business-Partners/Multi-Family-Developers/LIHTC-Program/2020-Competitive-Submissions-for-Website.pdf).
(2) The same kind log or table for the 2019 submissions. 
(3) A log or table of the MTBA firm applications received for round 2 of 2024 (equivalent to the posting, for round 1 of 2024, at https://dogvxws799i6n.cloudfront.net/wp-content/uploads/MTBA-Round-1-Applications.pdf).
(4) A log or table of the MTBA firm applications received for round 1 of 2023.
(5) A log or table of the MTBA firm applications received for round 2 of 2023.
(6) A log or table of the MTBA Closings in 2025 (equivalent to the posting, for the year 2024, at https://dogvxws799i6n.cloudfront.net/wp-content/uploads/2024-MTBA-Listing-for-Website-Closing_Carryforward-1.pdf).

Note that the first five of these documents pertain to applications submitted, not awards allocated. For context, also note that this request was preceded by an attempt to locate these documents on the THDA website. Files were successfully located for other years, but not for those requested above. For #4, a document was found, but the version on the website does not include a THDA # (project ID), which is unusual. The documents sought in this request are expected to include this field, if it at all exists.

In case any of the six logs of applications/closings only appear as part of a larger spreadsheet or database extract that covers several years or rounds, such a record would also be considered valid toward satisfying this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Kent Hoover, a citizen of Tennessee, in coordination with Lars Brown.

communications.subject: RE: Tennessee Public Records Act Request: LIHTC & MTBA Documents
communications.communication:
Mr. Hoover,

Our office received your email, regarding your request for records associated with a recent LIHTC project.  We can accommodate your request if it is in compliance with the Tennessee Public Records Act, found in Tennessee Code Annotated, Title 10, Chapter 7.  The policy states that the Requestor must be “a citizen of the State of Tennessee”. Our Public Records policy can be found at https://thda.org/about-thda/publicrecords

I have attached THDA’s Public Records Request Form which is needed to initiate the process.  We will need you to complete the Request Form, sign and date it.  We will also need proof in the form of a Photo ID issued by a governmental entity including the Requestor’s Tennessee address.  Please note, your request must be specific in nature. If requesting paper copies, the costs for any records and/or labor costs if applicable, will be disclosed to you after we determine the number of documents requested.

We will be happy to answer any questions you may have about this process.


Respectfully,

Jenia Tortora
Paralegal

Tennessee Housing Development Agency
Andrew Jackson Building
502 Deaderick Street, 3rd Floor
Nashville, Tennessee 37243
P. 615-815-2027

If Multifamily needs additional information after reviewing your request we will let you know as soon as possible. In accordance with our Public Records Policy, a response will be issued to you within seven (7) business days from the date your request was received. If the records cannot be provided to you within that time, we will let you know the reason for delay and give you an updated date of production.

If you have any additional questions, please feel free to contact our office. We look forward to working with you to timely process your request.

Thanks and have a great day!


Charity Miles Williams | Assistant Chief Legal Counsel

title: Records Request (Chesterfield County)

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Records Request (Chesterfield County)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I respectfully request access to and copies of all records related to the procurement of a school bus stop-arm camera system associated with: 

Solicitation/Bid: RFP # 25000109

Title/Description: School Bus Safety Systems

Issuing Entity: Chesterfield County School District

Specifically, I am requesting: 

All proposals and responses submitted by all vendors. 

All contracts, agreements, amendments, addenda, and pricing schedules resulting from this solicitation. 

All evaluation materials, including scoring sheets, evaluation committee notes or summaries, and recommendation memoranda. 

All bid tabulations, comparison matrices, or award justifications. 

All addenda to the solicitation, and any questions and answers (Q&A) or clarifications issued to vendors. 

Any other documentation or correspondence (including emails, letters, or internal memoranda) material to the evaluation, selection, and award of this stop-arm camera enforcement program. 

If any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please provide the non-exempt portions and cite the specific statutory basis for each withholding or redaction.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Jon Smith.

title: Central Point Police Department Flock Camera policy, invoice, map, and contracts

communications.subject: Oregon Public Records Law Request: Central Point Police Department Flock Camera policy, invoice, map, and contracts
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Oregon Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting a copy of the following records, that have already been requested to Central Point Police Department. By requesting previously produced records, I am hopeful that this request will require minimal labor to locate, review and produce the records. Please let me know if you need any additional information or clarification regarding the records I am seeking.

The records I am seeking are:

(1) all policies Central Point Police Department has that oversee and/or regulate the operation and use of Automated License Plate Readers, including the operation and use of Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares
(2) a copy of any and all invoices Central Point Police Department has between any and all Automated License Plate Readers softwares and programs, including Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares
(3) a map of all ALPR cameras that Central Point Police Department owns, operates or has access to
(4) a copy of any and all license and user agreements, contracts, memorandums of understanding or other similar type documents that describe and/or regulate the use, retention, collection, and sharing of ALPR data including but not limited to the agreements between Flock, Vigilant, Axon, or any similar ALPR programs and softwares

Please segregate all non-exempt material, and “black out” rather than “white out” any exempt material. Please cite specific exemptions in every instance that  the public body claims an exemption.

Thank you so much!

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Southern Oregon Flock Off

title: Military equipment (Laredo Police Department)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: Military equipment (Laredo Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

An inventory of all military equipment held by the department as of the date of fulfillment of this request. The request includes but is not limited to: 

(1) Unmanned, remotely piloted, powered aerial or ground vehicles.
(2) Mine-resistant ambush-protected (MRAP) vehicles or armored personnel carriers. However, police versions of standard consumer vehicles are specifically excluded from this subdivision.
(3) High mobility multipurpose wheeled vehicles (HMMWV), commonly referred to as Humvees, two and one-half-ton trucks, five-ton trucks, or wheeled vehicles that have a breaching or entry apparatus attached. However, unarmored all-terrain vehicles (ATVs) and motorized dirt bikes are specifically excluded from this subdivision.
(4) Tracked armored vehicles that provide ballistic protection to their occupants and utilize a tracked system instead of wheels for forward motion.
(5) Command and control vehicles that are either built or modified to facilitate the operational control and direction of public safety units.
(6) Weaponized aircraft, vessels, or vehicles of any kind.
(7) Battering rams, slugs, and breaching apparatuses that are explosive in nature. However, items designed to remove a lock, such as bolt cutters, or a handheld ram designed to be operated by one person, are specifically excluded from this subdivision.
(8) Firearms of .50 caliber or greater.
(9) Ammunition of .50 caliber or greater.
(10) Specialized firearms and ammunition of less than .50 caliber, including assault weapons as defined in Sections 30510 and 30515 of the Penal Code.
(11) Any firearm or firearm accessory that is designed to launch explosive projectiles.
(12) “Flashbang” grenades and explosive breaching tools, “tear gas,” and “pepper balls,” excluding standard, service-issued handheld pepper spray.
(13) Taser Shockwave, microwave weapons, water cannons, and the Long Range Acoustic Device (LRAD).
(14) The following projectile launch platforms and their associated munitions: 40mm projectile launchers, “bean bag,” rubber bullet, and specialty impact munition (SIM) weapons.
(15) Any other military equipment purchased by/for or transferred to the department. 

Requested components of the inventory:
The inventory should include the manufacturer, model number, quantity, initial costs of obtaining the equipment, and estimated annual costs of maintaining the equipment of each type of equipment, where "type" means each item shares the same manufacturer model number. 

The inventory should also specify under which program the equipment was obtained if applicable, including but not limited to the 1033 Program, the 1122 Program, the US Department of Homeland Security Grant Program, US Department of Justice’s Justice Assistant Grant Program, DOJ Equitable Sharing Program, US Department of the Treasury Forfeiture Fund’s Equitable Sharing Program, and The General Services Administration Federal Surplus Personal Property Donation program.  

I am also requesting a list of all military inventory that has gone missing in 2024 and 2025. 

I am a journalist employed by the news outlet The American Prospect and I am requesting a waiver of all fees. The disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of the tools available to the department to maintain safety and order, and protect against attacks from both domestic and outside sources.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Whitney Wimbish

communications.subject: Open Records Request :: W043391-010526
communications.communication:
Welcome to the City of Laredo Open Records Center. We have received your Public Information Request, and it has been diverted to the appropriate department for processing. Pursuant to the Public Information Act, the governmental body will try to complete the request within a reasonable amount of time depending on the nature of the request. However, you can expect to receive notification should the request require more than 10 business days to complete. 

Please note, the Texas Public Information Act does not require a governmental body to create new information, to do legal research, or to answer questions. The request must ask for records or information already in existence. In addition, the City of Laredo may collect a fee as prescribed by law or regulation for the requested documents. If a fee is not prescribed by law or regulation, the fee shall be the actual cost of duplicating the record(s) and any applicable labor costs. 

Finally, you can monitor the progress of your request through the automated open records center on the City of Laredo website .  Unfortunately, this email is an automated notification, which is unable to receive replies. Therefore, if you have any questions with respect to a pending request or pertaining to any open records issue,  you may directly contact this office through the open records center.  You may also reach us by phone at the number below. 

Very truly yours, 

City Attorney's Office 
Public Information Coordinator for the City of Laredo 
1110 Houston Street 
City Hall, 3rd Floor 
Laredo, Texas 78040 
(956) 791-7319 Telephone 
(956) 791-7494 Facsimile 

Track the issue status and respond at: https://laredo.mycusthelp.com/WEBAPP//_rs/RequestEdit.aspx?rid=43391

communications.subject: [Records Center] PD - Open Records Request :: W043391-010526
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
VIA E-MAIL 
Whitney  Wimbish 
MuckRock News, DEPT MR201076 
Boston, MA 02115 

RE: Public Information Request Reference No. W043391-010526 

Dear Whitney  Wimbish: 

The City of Laredo has received and processed your request for public information in which you asked for information regarding: Police- To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records: 

An inventory of all military equipment held by the department as of the date of fulfillment of this request. The request includes but is not limited to: 

(1) Unmanned, remotely piloted, powered aerial or ground vehicles. 
(2) Mine-resistant ambush-protected (MRAP) vehicles or armored personnel carriers. However, police versions of standard consumer vehicles are specifically excluded from this subdivision. 
(3) High mobility multipurpose wheeled vehicles (HMMWV), commonly referred to as Humvees, two and one-half-ton trucks, five-ton trucks, or wheeled vehicles that have a breaching or entry apparatus attached. However, unarmored all-terrain vehicles (ATVs) and motorized dirt bikes are specifically excluded from this subdivision. 
(4) Tracked armored vehicles that provide ballistic protection to their occupants and utilize a tracked system instead of wheels for forward motion. 
(5) Command and control vehicles that are either built or modified to facilitate the operational control and direction of public safety units. 
(6) Weaponized aircraft, vessels, or vehicles of any kind. 
(7) Battering rams, slugs, and breaching apparatuses that are explosive in nature. However, items designed to remove a lock, such as bolt cutters, or a handheld ram designed to be operated by one person, are specifically excluded from this subdivision. 
(8) Firearms of .50 caliber or greater. 
(9) Ammunition of .50 caliber or greater. 
(10) Specialized firearms and ammunition of less than .50 caliber, including assault weapons as defined in Sections 30510 and 30515 of the Penal Code. 
(11) Any firearm or firearm accessory that is designed to launch explosive projectiles. 
(12) “Flashbang” grenades and explosive breaching tools, “tear gas,” and “pepper balls,” excluding standard, service-issued handheld pepper spray. 
(13) Taser Shockwave, microwave weapons, water cannons, and the Long Range Acoustic Device (LRAD). 
(14) The following projectile launch platforms and their associated munitions: 40mm projectile launchers, “bean bag,” rubber bullet, and specialty impact munition (SIM) weapons. 
(15) Any other military equipment purchased by/for or transferred to the department. 

Requested components of the inventory: 
The inventory should include the manufacturer, model number, quantity, initial costs of obtaining the equipment, and estimated annual costs of maintaining the equipment of each type of equipment, where "type" means each item shares the same manufacturer model number. 

The inventory should also specify under which program the equipment was obtained if applicable, including but not limited to the 1033 Program, the 1122 Program, the US Department of Homeland Security Grant Program, US Department of Justice’s Justice Assistant Grant Program, DOJ Equitable Sharing Program, US Department of the Treasury Forfeiture Fund’s Equitable Sharing Program, and The General Services Administration Federal Surplus Personal Property Donation program. 

I am also requesting a list of all military inventory that has gone missing in 2024 and 2025. 

The City of Laredo strictly adheres to the provisions of the Texas Public Information Act, Texas Government Code Ch. 552, the interpretative opinions of the Texas Attorney General and the courts. Documents that have been released are available at the City of Laredo Open Records Center .  Select the option to "View My Open Records Request". 

If you have any questions or require any additional information, please do not hesitate to contact our office at (956) 791-7319. 

Sincerely, 
City Attorney's Office

title: Mason County, WV Opioid Settlements Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Mason County, WV Opioid Settlements Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Drew Solt 
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
March  24 2026

200 Sixth Street Suite 7
Point Pleasant, WV 25550

To Whom It May Concern, 
Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to County Name opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through today
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 through today
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds

If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records. 


If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you for considering my request. 
Sincerely, Drew Solt
drewsolt@yahoo.com
740-243-6793

title: UBC/Migrants deaths and remains found 2014-2025 (present) (Webb County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: UBC/Migrants deaths and remains found 2014-2025 (present) (Webb County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I am writing to request information pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 U.S.C. § 552. Specifically, I am seeking access to the following information regarding undocumented border crosser (UBC)/migrant remains that have been documented in your Medical Examiner Officer, Justice of the Peace, or County Sheriff's files for fiscal years 2014 - 2025 (present):

Name
Age
Sex
Country of origin
Condition of remains (e.g. fully or partially skeletonized)
Location of remains (preferably in GPS coordinates, alternatively any information available)
Cause of death
Discovering party
Additional notes/narrative

I understand that these records may contain sensitive information, and I assure you that any information provided will be used solely for legitimate research purposes.

Please inform me of any fees associated with this request, and I would be happy to provide payment if necessary. Additionally, please let me know if there are any limitations on the amount of information that can be provided, or if you require any further information from me in order to process this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,
Zoe Sigman
z.sigman@phd.hertie-school.org
PhD Researcher DYNAMICS
Berlin, Germany

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Zoe Sigman

title: Suffolk County police disciplinary files

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Suffolk County police disciplinary files
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

- Any and all lists, spreadsheets or other organized datasets that detail potential or substantiated allegations of misconduct and/or impeached or impeachable testimony, created under Brady v. Maryland and/or Giglio v. United States.
- Any and all records regarding substantiated allegations of misconduct or violations of internal department policy or criminal law by specific law enforcement officers.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

title: Marines support for 2023- Canadian documentary TV series "How I Got Here"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Marines support for 2023- Canadian documentary TV series "How I Got Here"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the US Marine Corps and the production of the Canadian TV documentary series "How I Got Here" (2023-present). According to a document released to me by the Marines through FOIA (see link below), the Marines provided support to this production. I therefore request any and all documents pertaining to Marine Corps support for "How I Got Here," including all episodes with which the Marines were involved. Such documents may include but should not be not limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of the Marines and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "How I Got Here," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

Here is a link to the aforementioned document:

https://app.box.com/s/t7tbqia68nc136qisb88aw4yvsoajzem

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation. These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Leadership and constituent emails (Hanover Clerk)

communications.subject: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Hanover Clerk)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Hampshire Right to Know Law, I hereby request the following records:

A copy of all email communications sent and received by the chair of the select board related to their elected position between Jan 1, 2026 and Jan 31, 2026, including but not limited to emails with constituents. This request is inclusive of all email sent to their official email, and all correspondence related to their elected position sent or received on personal and secondary accounts.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

In some cases, these records may be considered exempt from disclosure because of a privacy interest or some other concern. When considering whether a government record should be released, the courts routinely conduct a balancing test to weigh the public's interest in the records versus the government's interest in non-disclosure. We believe the public interest in these specific records is very high.

Nothing prevents you from making these records available to the public. Both the spirit and letter of the state's Right-to-Know law ensure "the greatest possible public access" to government records and proceedings. We ask that you consider this request carefully and conduct your own balancing test if you are inclined to deny any portion of this request.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen (Muckrock) and Jonathan Van Fleet (Concord Monitor)

communications.subject: FW: New Hampshire Right to Know Law Request: Leadership and constituent emails (Hanover Clerk)
communications.communication:
The Town will review your request and respond in accordance with RSA 91-A:4. To the extent responsive records are available, they will be made available for inspection by appointment during regular business hours, Monday through Friday, between 9:00 AM and 4:00 PM.

Please note the following regarding access to records:

  *   An appointment is required to review records in person.
  *   The Town does not transmit records electronically (including by email).
  *   All records must be reviewed and/or picked up in person at Town offices.
  *   If electronic records are requested, they will only be provided if a factory-sealed USB storage device (thumb drive) is supplied to the Town. Electronic records will be loaded onto that device and made available for in-person pickup.

If you wish to schedule an appointment or clarify your request, please contact me.
