title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Dickinson County)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Dickinson County)
communications.communication:
Dear Dickinson County Clerk FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Dickinson County Clerk county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Daily snapshots of the Motor Carrier Census file (also known as Company Census or MCS-150 data) for the period January 1, 2000  through January 27, 2026.   If daily snapshots are not available, I would be interested in monthly or quarterly snapshots.  

Fields of interest (standard census fields):                                                                                                       
  - DOT_NUMBER                                                                                                                                       
  - LEGAL_NAME, DBA_NAME                                                                                                                             
  - PHY_STREET, PHY_CITY, PHY_STATE, PHY_ZIP                                                                                                         
  - NBR_POWER_UNIT (power units)                                                                                                                     
  - DRIVER_TOTAL (drivers)                                                                                                                           
  - MCS150_MILEAGE, MCS150_MILEAGE_YEAR                                                                                                              
  - CARRIER_OPERATION (interstate/intrastate)                                                                                                        
  - CARGO types (CRGO_* fields)                                                                                                                      
  - Authority status fields                                                                                                                          
  - MCS150_DATE (filing date)                                                                                                                        

Preferred format: CSV, pipe-delimited text, or any machine-readable format.                                                                        

Please note that this request has been made numerous times per a review of FOIA logs, specifically FMCS-2023-05358, FMCS-2023-03393, FMCS-2023-03511 in FY2023, and also in previous years.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ian White

communications.subject: FW: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
Over the past year, FMCSA has improved the sharing of data with the public through the DOT - Public Data Portal<https://data.transportation.gov/> (Datahub).  By selecting "Trucking & Motorcoaches" you can search our many datasets.  You can use the search catalog in the upper right corner to type in key words like "Carrier" to see what kind of data we share with the public.   You are also able to search Company Census File which contains data for active, inactive an pending entities registered with FMCSA.

The FOIA provides that records made available under the FOIA's proactive disclosure provisions of subsection (a)(1) and (a)(2) are not themselves subject to individualized FOIA requests, see 5 U.S.C. § 552(a)(3)(A). This information is already made publicly available through the FMCSA Datahub.

After reviewing the data we share with the public, please let us know if there is something you are unable to find.

Thank you
FMCSA FOIA Office

Data I seek is daily snapshots of Motor Carrier Census file (also known as Company Census or MCS-150 data) for the period December 2023 through January 2026. I seek this data in CSV, pipe-delimited text, or any machine-readable format.

Please note this archived data is NOT published by the FMCSA, DOT or any third party. I have searched on the Public Data Portal and it is not available. This is data which was once published, and taken offline after it was replaced by a more current version.

Thank you and please let me know if you have questions.

communications.subject: Re: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,
In response to your request for end of year snapshot data, please see the
links below.  End of year data for 2025 is currently available on the
Datahub for public access.

Please note that these files are too large for standard applications like
Excel or Notepad, as each contains between 1.5 million records.  To view
these files, you will need data analysis tools such as Python, R, or SAS or
a specialized large-file text editor.

FMCSA_Census_2023: https://public.huddle.com/b/wWEZMl/index.html

FMCSA_Census_2024: https://public.huddle.com/b/oDkWNY/index.html

Please let me know if you have any questions.

Thank you
-- 

Jennifer Weatherly

Director, Office of Information Services

Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration

1200 New Jersey Avenue, SE | Washington, DC 20590

M: (202) 420-0106

Jennifer.Weatherly@dot.gov

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request: Untitled
communications.communication:
Hi Jennifer

Thank you for your response.  Thank you for providing the two census snapshot files (CENSUS_FILE_231229 and CENSUS_FILE_20241227). We reviewed both files against the publicly available SODA Company Census File (data.transportation.gov, dataset az4n-8mr2) and found four issues that prevent us from using  the snapshots as delivered.

- Issue 1: Power unit counts are classification codes, not integers. The TOT_PWR field contains letter codes (A through Z) for 54% of rows  instead of numeric power unit counts. The SODA Company Census File provides clean integer values for the same carriers in the  NBR_POWER_UNIT field. Large carriers such as UPS (DOT 21800) and Walmart (DOT 63585) return empty or coded values in the delivered files  but show 28,307 and 13,618 power units respectively in the SODA table. We need the actual integer count.

- Issue 2: Mileage year is a date string, not a year. The MLG150_YEAR field contains date-formatted strings (eg "01-JUN-74") for 100% of  rows. The SODA table provides this as a 4-digit year integer (eg 2023). We need a 4-digit year matching the SODA field  MCS150_MILEAGE_YEAR.

- Issue 3: Mileage is missing for 17% of rows. The MLG150 field is empty for carriers that have populated mileage values in the SODA table,  including large national carriers. We need MLG150 populated for all carriers that have mileage in the SODA sms_input_census table.

- Issue 4: Header row truncated at 1024 bytes. The tab-delimited header is cut at exactly 1024 bytes, splitting the column name "TRMHMTRUCK"  across two lines. The file has 163 data columns but the header shows 93 on the first line and 71 on the second. We need the header  exported with no line-length limit.

- December 2025 snapshot. Your office indicated that a December 2025 snapshot is available on the DOT DataHub. We checked and this is not  the case. The Company Census File on DataHub (dataset az4n-8mr2) is a live, daily-updating dataset that reflects the current state of the  census as of today. It is not a historical snapshot archive. There is no December 2025 point-in-time file available on that platform.

The delivered files do contain usable data for DOT numbers, legal names, states, driver counts and operating status. The power unit and  mileage fields are the two we cannot use in their current format.

Thank you again for your diligence and support, I really appreciate it.

   - Link to 2025 Data: https://public.huddle.com/b/aEpwLG/index.html


[image: image.png]
-- 

Jennifer Weatherly

Director, Office of Information Services

1200 New Jersey Avenue, SE | Washington, DC 20590

title: Maryland DPSCS Adult Room and Board + Medical Copay Policy

communications.subject: Maryland Public Information Act Request: Maryland DPSCS Adult Room and Board + Medical Copay Policy
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Maryland Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The following materials regarding the imposition, assessment, collection and balances of inmate fees and debt:

a) All current policies and procedures regarding medical copays and room & board costs or fees for which inmates are or may become liable. Please include all policies and procedures related to how fees are set, accrued, imposed, collected, and enforced (such as those related to per diem fees, ability to pay determinations, penalties for late or non-payment, authorization for civil or criminal action for payment enforcement, etc).

b) All reports, databases, spreadsheets, or equivalent materials regarding total fees collected from inmates for medical copays and room and board fees from 2020-2024. Please include the number of individuals charged for medical and room & board costs or fees and the total amount of medical and room & board fees imposed this past calendar year. Please also include the total amount of room and board fees collected through the work-release program.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jenny Wadhwa

title: RAW RIPA Data (Monterey Park Police Departmentt)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: RAW RIPA Data (Monterey Park Police Departmentt)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hello, 

I am requesting your department's state mandated RIPA stop data from 2020 to today's date. Please upload in .csv or Excel format. No PDFs. 

Thank you

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

title: Toxicology Report: G. Vargas

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I need a toxicology report for Gustavo Billanueva Vargas, DOB 01/16/1961. He was killed in an automobile accident on 01/11/2026 in Fresno Ca on HWY 99S.

I am attempting to file a claim for his accidental death life insurance policy. I need the toxicology report for this claim.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Myra Askew

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Toxicology Report: G. Vargas
communications.communication:
Hello, 

Please find the request form attached. Thank you for your help with this request. 

Sincerely, 
MuckRock

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Toxicology Report: G. Vargas
communications.communication:
Hi Kim, 

I have not received the documents just yet. Are they being sent by mail? If so, I’d like to confirm my address 3858 Robinson Ave, Clovis, CA 93619. 

Is the document being sent over the toxicology report? 

Thank you, 
Myra Askew

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Toxicology Report: G. Vargas
communications.communication:
Myra, there seems to be a misunderstanding. Unfortunately, the reports that were listed on a long list and already paid for were mailed out to MuckRock. Gustavo’s isn’t anywhere near being done. Most cases take about 6 months on average to finish. I also don’t show that we have received the payment for it. Nothing can be done without a request and payment.

Kimberly Lopez (Kim)
Program Technician
Fresno County Sheriff’s Department
Office of the Public Administrator
Coroner’s Office
559-600-8669 (phone) M/W-Th
559-600-8613 (phone) T/Fri
559-600-8319 (fax)
559-600-8660 (PA main phone)
­kimberly.lopez@fresnosheriff.org<mailto:kimberly.lopez@fresnosheriff.org>

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Toxicology Report: G. Vargas
communications.communication:
Hello, 

Thank you so much for following up. I was confused because I previously spoke to someone with the coroner’s office and they did advise that the report could take up to 6 months (and they also advised of the fee), and I would need to send in a form as well.

My confusion stemmed from the notification from Muckrock, but I will proceed with my request directly through the coroner’s office via the applicable form and corresponding fee.

Thank you for your attention.

Respectfully, 
Myra Askew

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Toxicology Report: G. Vargas
communications.communication:
Sounds good. Thanks.

Kimberly Lopez (Kim)

title: Attn: Ohio Open Records Law request about Flock Safety (Stark County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Ohio Open Records Law Request: Attn: Ohio Open Records Law request about Flock Safety (Stark County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Ohio Open Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

All records referencing the department’s relationship with the company Flock Safety to install automated license-plate readers (ALPRs), including but not limited to the following:

- Flock transparency portals and reports
- Flock services agreement(s)
- Purchase order(s)
- Flock network audits
- Search audits
- Flock logs
- Contract(s) between the department and Flock
- Flock sales pitches and recruitment materials
- Meeting minutes
- Training materials, including but not limited to presentations, powerpoint slides, and videos
- Department regulations referencing the Flock system and/or license plate readers
- Any and all communications, including but not limited to emails, email attachments, text messages, voice mail messages, between agency officials and Flock employees

When making this request please make sure to search the following key terms:

- “@flocksafety.com” 

When making this request please exclude newsletters, automated notifications, Google alerts, or other mass-distributed messages.

I would like these records in electronic format, transmitted via email or other digital method; please refrain from sending paper copies of the records. If you anticipate that providing me with all responsive records will take longer than two weeks, I ask that you provide records to me on a rolling basis as they become available. In any event, do not destroy any records that may potentially contain responsive documents.
 
If you determine that any of the information I’ve requested qualifies for an exemption from disclosure, I ask you to note whether the exemption is discretionary, and if so whether it is necessary in this case to exercise your discretion to withhold the information. In any event, please provide a signed notification citing the legal authorities on which you rely if you determine that any of the information is exempt and will not be disclosed.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Frank Miciano

title: Banned books list (Department of Corrections)

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Banned books list (Department of Corrections)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

A list of all books and other printed publications that are banned or otherwise not permitted in prisons. Please include the title and author of the publication. If available, please include the reasons for prohibiting the publication, as well as the date of the decision.

If possible, please send this information in a searchable spreadsheet format, such as an Excel file or a CSV file.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Dillon Bergin

title: ORIS docs

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: ORIS docs
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Copies of ORIS User Manual, ORIS DBA User Manual, ORIS System Integration Test Procedures and IPS System Design Specifications, documents which are described in CIA-RDP93B01194R001700040017-5.

I am a member of the news media and request classification as such. I have previously written about the government and its activities, with some reaching over 100,000 readers in outlets such as MuckRock, Motherboard, Property of the People, AND Magazine, Unicorn Riot, and more. As such, as I have a reasonable expectation of publication and my editorial and writing skills are well established. In addition, I discuss and comment on the files online and make them available through non-profits such as the library Internet Archive and and the journalist non-profit, MuckRock, disseminating them to a large audience. While my research is not limited to this, a great deal of it, including this, focuses on the activities and attitudes of the government itself. As such, it is not necessary for me to demonstrate the relevance of this particular subject in advance.

Additionally, case law states that “proof of the ability to disseminate the released information to a broad cross- section of the public is not required.” Judicial Watch, Inc. v. Dep’t of Justice, 365 F.3d 1108, 1126 (D.C. Cir. 2004); see Carney v. U.S. Dep’t of Justice, 19 F.3d 807, 814-15 (2d Cir. 1994). Further, courts have held that "qualified because it also had “firm” plans to “publish a number of . . . ‘document sets’” concerning United States foreign and national security policy." Under this criteria, as well, I qualify as a member of the news media.

Additionally, courts have held that the news media status "focuses on the nature of the requester, not its request. The provision requires that the request be “made by” a representative of the news media. Id. § 552(a)(4)(A)(ii)(II). A newspaper reporter, for example, is a representative of the news media regardless of how much interest there is in the story for which he or she is requesting information." As such, the details of the request itself are moot for the purposes of determining the appropriate fee category.

As my primary purpose is to inform about government activities by reporting on it and making the raw data available, I request that fees be waived.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Emma Best

title: Pittsburg, KS CJIS Audits

communications.subject: The Kansas Open Records Act Request: Pittsburg, KS CJIS Audits
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Kansas Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

The last three audits of the city's MULES and NCICS systems conducted under CJIS standards or other requirements. This should include dates, reports, anomalies investigated, whether such anomalies were cleared or sustained, pass/fail results for the department, and the auditor who performed the audit.

The metadata from the file system where these audits reside.  This data is to include, file name, creation time, modify time, and other fields.  We  request this data be provided in screenshot form from the system where the files reside. The IT department will definitely be able to assist with this very brief task if it is not understood by the recipient.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael

communications.subject: RE: The Kansas Open Records Act Request: Pittsburg, KS CJIS Audits
communications.communication: I may be interested in narrowing the scope of this request.  It is unclear to me where the burden is coming from.  I expected that I was requesting existing documents the city keeps on hand.  I would not expect that retrieving three existing audits would require 5 hours of research time.  Can you explain the source of the burden so that I may adjust my request if desired?

communications.subject: Re: The Kansas Open Records Act Request: Pittsburg, KS CJIS Audits
communications.communication:
Good morning, the requested NCIC records are not maintained by the city and were only accessible through Kansas Highway Patrol's CJIS system. The Kansas Highway Patrol is the NCIC regulatory agency for the State of Kansas. City staff took five hours to research (including telephone time with highway patrol staff) and locate the requested records through the highway patrol's system and then redact any personal case identifying information from those records.

Thanks.


[cid:image007.png@01D2D91A.42328570]

Alexus Winder

Records administrator

Pittsburg Police Department

Beard-Shanks Law Enforcement Center

620-235-0400 – Administration

620-231-1700 – Dispatch

[twitter]<https://twitter.com/PittPD>[fb]<https://www.facebook.com/Pittsburg-Police-Department-373669182718883/>[cid:image008.png@01D2D91A.42328570]<https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC1gjSpZPdXTpQSrg3L99RtA>

title: Dataset: 911 Call Data (Irvine Police Department Communications Bureau, Irvine, Ca, Usa)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

RAW Computer Aided Dispatch Data queried out of your emergency phone system on 911 calls for service from January 2014 to the most recent date data is collected. In CSV, TXT, or XML format, a machine-readable spreadsheet (not XLSX format). 

Here are the column headers which I am requesting:

Master Incident Number or Event Number – Unique numerical identifier of the call for service
Case Number – If an incident report was written, this is the file number, which can be joined to the incidents and arrests datasets
Call Date – Date the call for service was generated
Call Time – Time the call for service was generated
Source – How the call was generated from the public, or self-initiated by an officer
Priority – The priority level of the call response, with ‘P’ being the highest, followed in order numerically
Nature – The description of the call for service.
Address – The block number and street or intersection of the call for service.
Latitude/longitude - coordinates for call of service
X – Mapping coordinate of the call for service
Y – Mapping coordinate of the call for service
District – The patrol district where the call for service was located.
Beat – The patrol beat where the call for service was located, which is a sub-division of the district
Tract – The census tract where the incident occurred, based on 2010 census data
Disposition – How the call for service was closed
Cancelled – Whether the call for service was cancelled prior to officer arrival
Dispatch Date and Time - The exact date and time when the dispatcher sent the call to emergency responders, such as police, fire, or EMS
Entry Date and Time - The date and time when the call was initially logged into the 911 computer-aided dispatch (CAD) system
Received Date and Time - The date and time when the 911 call was first answered by the dispatch center
Enroute Date and Time - The date and time when the assigned responder (e.g., a patrol car, ambulance, or fire truck) began traveling to the incident location
On Scene Date and Time - The date and time when the responder arrived at the scene of the incident
Close Date and Time - The date and time when the incident or call was marked as completed or resolved in the CAD system
Dispatcher ID - A unique identifier for the dispatcher who handled and managed the 911 call. This may be used for tracking or auditing purposes

If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Filed Requests

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Dataset: 911 Call Data (Irvine Police Department Communications Bureau, Irvine, Ca, Usa)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Upon review, additional data fields are necessary to fully interpret the records already requested and/or produced. I therefore request the following additional column headers, to the extent they exist in your CAD system, for the same date range (January 2014 to the most recent date data is collected), in CSV, TXT, or XML format:

* Unique Identifier – A unique numerical identifier for each call for service, if distinct from the master incident or event number
* Dispatcher ID(s) – Identifier(s) for all dispatchers who handled the call, if multiple
* Responder ID – A unique identifier for the responding unit(s) or team(s)
* Prime Unit – Identifier indicating the primary response unit
* Other Units – All additional responding unit identifiers
* Back-up Requested – Indicator or code showing whether back-up was requested
* Transport – Indicator (Y/N) of whether transport occurred
* Transport Start and End Times – If transport occurred
* Transport Destination Address – The address or facility to which transport occurred
* Program or Response Type – Any field identifying non-police, co-response, or alternative response teams (including behavioral health, crisis, or care-based units), if applicable

If any of the above fields are stored under different names, codes, or tables, I request the functional equivalent fields.

I also request any available data dictionary or codebook necessary to interpret these fields.

If you deny any portion of this supplemental request, please cite the specific statutory exemption relied upon and provide the applicable appeal procedures as required by law.

As before, these records are requested for non-commercial purposes and will be made available to the general public. Please inform me in advance of any fees associated with fulfilling this request.

Thank you for your cooperation.

Sincerely,
FR

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Dataset: 911 Call Data (Irvine Police Department Communications Bureau, Irvine, Ca, Usa)
communications.communication:
Good Evening,

In reference to your request under the California Public Records Act (Government Code Section 7920 et seq.), additional time is required for consultation and review, which shall be conducted with all practicable speed. under the California Public Records Act (Government Code Section 7922.525(b) et seq.), an extension of an additional 14 days is appropriate under these circumstances.

Based on the initial 10 day response period and the additional extension periods, our office will contact you on or before February 7, 2026 regarding a response of the requested records.

Thank you,

[Image]
Kailene Loza | Lead Records Specialist
Irvine Police Department | Public Safety Records

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Dataset: 911 Call Data (Irvine Police Department Communications Bureau, Irvine, Ca, Usa)
communications.communication:
Subject: Re: California Public Records Act Request: Dataset: 911 Call Data (Irvine Police Department Communications Bureau)

Dear Ms. Loza,

Thank you for your continued updates. However, I want to note that this request was filed on January 14, 2026, and no records have been produced to date.

Under the California Public Records Act, an agency must respond within 10 calendar days (Gov. Code § 7922.535), with one permissible 14-day extension for specified reasons (§ 7922.525(b)). That extension period expired on February 7, 2026. The subsequent extensions — to February 14, February 28, March 14, and now March 28 — are not authorized by statute. The CPRA does not provide for indefinitely chained extensions in lieu of production or a formal denial.

I am not aware of any records having been produced or any exemption having been invoked for any portion of this request. If the City intends to withhold any responsive records, Government Code § 7922.530 requires a written determination identifying the specific exemption and the name and title of the person responsible for the denial.

I ask that the City either produce responsive records or provide a written exemption determination by March 28, 2026. If records are still being processed, please provide a specific accounting of what remains, why production has not occurred in the more than two months since this request was filed, and a firm production date.

If this request is not resolved by March 28, I will consider my options under Government Code § 7923.000, including initiating a proceeding to compel disclosure.

Thank you,
Filed Requests

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Dataset: 911 Call Data (Irvine Police Department Communications Bureau, Irvine, Ca, Usa)
communications.communication:
Good Afternoon,

The City anticipates producing records on or before March 28, 2026. Please understand the data requested is for sixteen years and is currently being searched through multiple systems. To provide perspective on the data being gathered, the Irvine Police Department receives approximately 8,300 – 10,400 Calls for Service monthly. Additionally, the Irvine Police Department continues to receive an increased amount of Public Records Act requests daily.

Thank you,

[Image]

title: Flock Safety search audits (San Jose Police Department)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety search audits (San Jose Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to the department’s Flock Safety system:
1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of March 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of March 1, 2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. A list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable. Please do not redirect me to your transparency portal.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Gerardo Leon

Via Electronic Mail: 205734-01832273@requests.muckrock.com<mailto:205734-01832273@requests.muckrock.com>

MuckRock News DEPT MR205734

263 Huntington Ave

Boston, MA 02115

Dear Leslie:
Reference: DOI-2026-004129
Subject: Freedom of Information Act Request
This letter is to acknowledge receipt of your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request submitted on 2/24/26 and received by the National Park Service on 2/24/26 in which you requested:
All complaints regarding Herbert Hoover NHS received through the park’s email portal from January 1st, 2021 to February 23rd, 2026
It has been assigned FOIA Number DOI-2026-004129. Any correspondence, with this office, regarding this request should cite this number.
Scope of Request Unclear—43 C.F.R. § 2.5<http://www.ecfr.gov/cgi-bin/text-idx?c=ecfr&sid=0fc3ab3499768eebc2e3691c8cf88dec&rgn=div5&view=text&node=43:1.1.1.1.2&idno=43#43:1.1.1.1.2.2.5.3>
The FOIA requires that requests describe the records sought with sufficient detail to allow an agency employee familiar with the subject area of the request to locate the records with a reasonable amount of effort. Your request does not adequately describe the records sought; therefore, we are unable to process your request at this time. If you wish to pursue your request, please describe the records you seek sufficiently to enable a professional employee familiar with the subject to locate the existing records with a reasonable effort. Please expound upon what you mean by complaints. For example are you seeking law enforcement complaints or complaints submitted by visitors about various things?
According to our regulations, if we do not receive your written response clarifying what records you are looking for within 20 workdays from the date of this communication we will presume that you are no longer interested in pursuing your request. In such a circumstance, we will not be able to comply with your request and will close our file on it. See 43 C.F.R. § 2.5(d)<https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-43/subtitle-A/part-2#2.5>. You may seek assistance from the designated FOIA contact or FOIA Requester Center at https://www.doi.gov/foia/foiacenters.

Multitrack Processing—43 C.F.R. §§ 2.14<https://www.doi.gov/foia/news/guidance?c=ecfr&sid=0fc3ab3499768eebc2e3691c8cf88dec&rgn=div5&view=text&node=43:1.1.1.1.2&idno=43#43:1.1.1.1.2.4.5.1>, .15<http://www.ecfr.gov/cgi-bin/text-idx?c=ecfr&sid=0fc3ab3499768eebc2e3691c8cf88dec&rgn=div5&view=text&node=43:1.1.1.1.2&idno=43#43:1.1.1.1.2.4.5.2>
We use Multitrack Processing to process FOIA requests. The Simple track is for requests that would generally take one to five workdays to process. The Normal track is for requests that would generally take six to twenty workdays to process. The Complex track is for requests that would generally take twenty-one to sixty workdays to process. The Extraordinary track is for requests that would generally take more than sixty workdays to process. The Expedited track is for requests that have been granted expedited processing, which are processed as soon as practicable. Within each track, requests are usually processed on a first-in, first-out basis.
Your request falls into the Normal processing track. There are 65 requests pending ahead of yours in this processing track.

Appeal Rights—43 C.F.R. §§ 2.57<https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-43/subtitle-A/part-2?c=ecfr&sid=0fc3ab3499768eebc2e3691c8cf88dec&rgn=div5&view=text&node=43:1.1.1.1.2&idno=43#43:1.1.1.1.2.8.5.1>, .58<http://www.ecfr.gov/cgi-bin/text-idx?c=ecfr&sid=0fc3ab3499768eebc2e3691c8cf88dec&rgn=div5&view=text&node=43:1.1.1.1.2&idno=43#43:1.1.1.1.2.8.5.2>, .59<http://www.ecfr.gov/cgi-bin/text-idx?c=ecfr&sid=0fc3ab3499768eebc2e3691c8cf88dec&rgn=div5&view=text&node=43:1.1.1.1.2&idno=43#43:1.1.1.1.2.8.5.3>
You may appeal this response to the Department’s FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer. If you choose to appeal, the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer must receive your FOIA appeal no later than 90 workdays from the date of this communication. Appeals arriving or delivered after 5 p.m. Eastern Time, Monday through Friday, will be deemed received on the next workday.
Your appeal must be made in writing. You may submit your appeal and accompanying materials to the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer by mail, courier service, fax, or email. All communications concerning your appeal should be clearly marked with the words: "FREEDOM OF INFORMATION APPEAL." You must include an explanation of why you believe this response is in error. You must also include with your appeal copies of all correspondence between you and the NPS concerning your FOIA request, including your original FOIA request and this response. Failure to include with your appeal all correspondence between you and the NPS will result in the Department's rejection of your appeal, unless the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer determines (in the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer’s sole discretion) that good cause exists to accept the defective appeal.
Please include your name and daytime telephone number (or the name and telephone number of an appropriate contact), email address and fax number (if available) in case the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer needs additional information or clarification of your appeal.
DOI FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Office Contact Information

1849 C Street, N.W.

Fax: 202-208-6677
Email: FOIA.Appeals@sol.doi.gov<http://www.ecfr.gov/cgi-bin/text-idx>
If you have any questions or concerns please feel free to contact me at mwr_foia@nps.gov.



Sincerely,
Helen Smith

communications.communication:
Ms. Smith, 

Thanks for the opportunity to clarify. 

The National Park Service provides guidance on how to file a complaint here: https://www.nps.gov/orgs/1358/submit-a-complaint.htm. There, the Service says "If you would like to file a general complaint about park operations, or about an employee that is not law enforcement, contact the park through their official webpage. Find a park at www.nps.gov/findapark then click the Contact Us link near the bottom."
 
I am seeking complaints regarding Herbert Hoover NHS received through the park’s email portal, found at https://www.nps.gov/heho/contacts.htm, from January 1st, 2021 to February 23rd, 2026. This may include complaints submitted by visitors about various things or complaints about law enforcement. If they are complaints, and they were submitted via the Herbert Hoover NHS email portal, that's what I'm looking for.

Hope that clarifies!

Patrick (not Leslie)

communications.subject: Re: FOIA DOI-2026-004129
communications.communication:
April 8, 2026


Patrick File

Via Electronic Mail: 205734-01832273@requests.muckrock.com<mailto:205734-01832273@requests.muckrock.com>

MuckRock News DEPT MR205734

263 Huntington Ave

Boston, MA 02115

Dear Patrick:
Reference: DOI-2026-004129
Subject: Freedom of Information Act Request
This letter is our final response to your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request submitted on 2/24/26 and received by the National Park Service on 2/24/26 in which you requested:
All complaints regarding Herbert Hoover NHS received through the park’s email portal from January 1st, 2021 to February 23rd, 2026
It has been assigned FOIA Number DOI-2026-004129. Any correspondence, with this office, regarding this request should cite this number.
We have attached one document containing one page, which is being released to you in part.  Portions of these materials are being withheld under the following FOIA Exemption:

Exemption 6—43 C.F.R. §§ 2.23<http://www.ecfr.gov/cgi-bin/text-idx?c=ecfr&sid=0fc3ab3499768eebc2e3691c8cf88dec&rgn=div5&view=text&node=43:1.1.1.1.2&idno=43#se43.1.2_123>, .24<https://www.doi.gov/sites/doi.gov/files/uploads/FOIA%20Handbook%20-%20Final%20-%20signed.pdf?c=ecfr&sid=0fc3ab3499768eebc2e3691c8cf88dec&rgn=div5&view=text&node=43:1.1.1.1.2&idno=43#se43.1.2_124>
Exemption 6 allows an agency to withhold “personnel and medical files and similar files the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy.” 5 U.S.C. § 552(b)(6)<http://www.ecfr.gov/cgi-bin/text-idx>. We are withholding one page in part under Exemption 6.
The phrase “similar files” covers any agency records containing information about a particular individual that can be identified as applying to that individual. To determine whether releasing records containing information about a particular individual would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy, we are required to balance the privacy interest that would be affected by disclosure against any public interest in the information.
Under the FOIA, the only relevant public interest to consider under the exemption is the extent to which the information sought would shed light on an agency’s performance of its statutory duties or otherwise let citizens ‘know what their government is up to.’ The burden is on the requester to establish that disclosure would serve the public interest. When the privacy interest at stake and the public interest in disclosure have been determined, the two competing interests must be weighed against one another to determine which is the greater result of disclosure: the harm to personal privacy or the benefit to the public. The purposes for which the request for information is made do not impact this balancing test, as a release of information requested under the FOIA constitutes a release to the general public.
The information that has been withheld under Exemption 6 consists of personal information, and we have determined that the individuals to whom this information pertains have a substantial privacy interest in withholding it. Additionally, you have not provided information that explains a relevant public interest under the FOIA in the disclosure of this personal information and we have determined that the disclosure of this information would shed little or no light on the performance of the agency’s statutory duties. Because the harm to personal privacy is greater than whatever public interest may be served by disclosure, release of the information would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of the privacy of these individuals and we are withholding it under Exemption 6.
Helen Smith, National Park Service, Government Information Specialist is responsible for this denial.
The Office of the Solicitor's Division of General Law and the Departmental FOIA Office FOIA Bulletin 22-02, Preapproval Withholdings was the subject of consultation regarding this withholding.

Appeal Rights—43 C.F.R. §§ 2.57<https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-43/subtitle-A/part-2?c=ecfr&sid=0fc3ab3499768eebc2e3691c8cf88dec&rgn=div5&view=text&node=43:1.1.1.1.2&idno=43#43:1.1.1.1.2.8.5.1>, .58<http://www.ecfr.gov/cgi-bin/text-idx?c=ecfr&sid=0fc3ab3499768eebc2e3691c8cf88dec&rgn=div5&view=text&node=43:1.1.1.1.2&idno=43#43:1.1.1.1.2.8.5.2>, .59<http://www.ecfr.gov/cgi-bin/text-idx?c=ecfr&sid=0fc3ab3499768eebc2e3691c8cf88dec&rgn=div5&view=text&node=43:1.1.1.1.2&idno=43#43:1.1.1.1.2.8.5.3>
You may appeal this response to the Department’s FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer. If you choose to appeal, the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer must receive your FOIA appeal no later than 90 workdays from the date of this final response. Appeals arriving or delivered after 5 p.m. Eastern Time, Monday through Friday, will be deemed received on the next workday.
Your appeal must be made in writing. You may submit your appeal and accompanying materials to the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer by mail, courier service, fax, or email. All communications concerning your appeal should be clearly marked with the words: "FREEDOM OF INFORMATION APPEAL." You must include an explanation of why you believe this response is in error. You must also include with your appeal copies of all correspondence between you and the NPS concerning your FOIA request, including your original FOIA request and this response. Failure to include with your appeal all correspondence between you and the NPS will result in the Department's rejection of your appeal, unless the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer determines (in the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer’s sole discretion) that good cause exists to accept the defective appeal.
Please include your name and daytime telephone number (or the name and telephone number of an appropriate contact), email address and fax number (if available) in case the FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Officer needs additional information or clarification of your appeal.
DOI FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Office Contact Information
Department of the Interior
Office of the Solicitor
1849 C Street, N.W.
MS-6556 MIB
Washington, DC 20240
Attn: FOIA/Privacy Act Appeals Office

Telephone: 202-208-5339
Fax: 202-208-6677
Email: FOIA.Appeals@sol.doi.gov<http://www.ecfr.gov/cgi-bin/text-idx>
Conclusion for Final Response
For your information, Congress excluded three discrete categories of law enforcement and national security records from the requirements of FOIA. See 5 U.S.C. 552(c)<https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-43/subtitle-A/part-2>. This response is limited to those records that are subject to the requirements of FOIA. This is a standard notification that is given to all our requesters and should not be taken as an indication that excluded records do, or do not, exist.
The 2007 FOIA amendments created the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) to offer mediation services to resolve disputes between FOIA requesters and Federal agencies as a non-exclusive alternative to litigation. Using OGIS services does not affect your right to pursue litigation. You may contact OGIS in any of the following ways:
Office of Government Information Services
National Archives and Records Administration
8601 Adelphi Road - OGIS
College Park, MD 20740-6001
E-mail: ogis@nara.gov<http://www.ecfr.gov/cgi-bin/text-idx>
<http://www.ecfr.gov/cgi-bin/text-idx>Web: https://www.archives.gov/ogis
Telephone: 202-741-5770
Toll-free: 1-877-684-6448
Please note that using OGIS services does not affect the timing of filing an appeal with the Department’s FOIA & Privacy Act Appeals Officer. Contact information for the Department’s FOIA Public Liaison, who you may also seek dispute resolution services from, is available at https://www.doi.gov/foia/foiacenters.
If you have any questions about our response to your request, you may contact me by email at mwr_foia@nps.gov.


Sincerely,
Helen Smith

FOIA Officer

National Park Service, Department of the Interior Regions 3, 4, 5

MWR_FOIA@nps.gov

This message was sent by the MWR FOIA office.

title: FBI support for 2012 TV documentary "Killer Profile: Bobby Joe Long"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: FBI support for 2012 TV documentary "Killer Profile: Bobby Joe Long"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to FBI support for or collaboration with the production of the 2012 TV documentary "Killer Profile: Bobby Joe Long," directed by Phil Nobile, Jr. Such documents may include, but should not be limited to, script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of the FBI and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Killer Profile: Bobby Joe Long," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication. I request a search of all locations where such documents might be located, including but not limited to the entertainment liaison offices and communications HQ or similar offices.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation. These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: RE: Freedom of Information Act Request #1676613-000
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern,

This message pertains to FOIA request #1676613-000.

I am writing to appeal the determination that my request does not "demonstrate that the requested information is in the public interest because it is likely to contribute significantly to public understanding of the operations and activities of the government."

As I explain in my original FOIA request, I am the Director of Research Operations at a non-profit organization that investigates, among other things, the relationship between Hollywood and the federal government. Besides the fact that they will be available to the public, these and other records thus made available will, we hope and expect, be referenced in research papers and other writings, and the mere availability of them makes the public aware of an entire field of government operations (namely, collaboration with Hollywood) of which the public is largely not aware. This increased awareness, in turn, has the potential to transform the way the public views and is affected by entertainment media. Furthermore, I am the editor-in-chief of a research magazine published by said foundation called "Cultural Engineering Studies," to which I also contribute articles (including a recent article on NASA's relationship to Hollywood). The magazine explores, among other topics, Hollywood's relationship to the federal government, and such documents indeed are cited in the magazine.

Respectfully,

Brett Carollo

title: Records Pertaining to Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (“KB Reid”) (United States Department of the Army)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Records Pertaining to Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (“KB Reid”) (United States Department of the Army)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This request seeks all records from 1995 (or earlier) to 2010 (or later if available) referencing Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (also known as “KB Reid”), a U.S. Army Military Intelligence officer who was selected for the Military Intelligence Excepted Career Program (GREAT SKILL) in 1987 and subsequently served in some of the most sensitive Army and interagency HUMINT roles of the post–Cold War and post-9/11 era.

I respectfully request:

	1.	Personnel Records
	•	Official Military Personnel File (OMPF), Officer Evaluation Reports (OERs), commendations, promotions, awards, fitness reports, assignment orders, and internal performance evaluations for Colonel Kurush Bharucha-Reid (SSN/Service Number can be provided if required).
	•	Decorations including: Defense Superior Service Medal, Bronze Star (with Oak Leaf Cluster), Defense Meritorious Service Medal (x3), Meritorious Service Medal (x3), Joint Service Commendation Medal, Army Commendation Medal (with Oak Leaf Cluster), Army Achievement Medal, Ranger tab, Special Forces tab, Pathfinder, Master Parachutist, and Military Free Fall Jump Master badges ￼.
	2.	Program and Command Assignments
	•	All records documenting his service in the GREAT SKILL Program, including appointment, reassignment memos, or internal assessments.
	•	His service as Regional Desk Officer and Special Assistant for Military Affairs at a national-level intelligence agency, with a focus on East Asian and Near East operations.
	•	Records from his time as Commander of a Defense HUMINT Service base in Bosnia, and as CJ2 for Combined Joint Task Force Fervent Archer.
	•	Documentation from his command of a sensitive HUMINT collection detachment deployed to Afghanistan (2002), where he served as the first HUMINT case officer supporting U.S. Special Operations Command (USSOCOM).
	•	Records from his second Afghanistan deployment (2004), where he commanded a HUMINT base supporting surge operations during Afghanistan’s first democratic elections.
	•	Internal records, rosters, orders, or awards tied to his 2005 Iraq deployment as a Senior HUMINT Advisor in a sensitive interagency coordination cell.
	3.	Strategic Instruction & Final Command
	•	Records of his time as Chief of the Military Group and Senior DoD Instructor at a joint interagency strategic intelligence training institution (likely DIA or a multi-agency DoD school).
	•	Materials from his command of the U.S. Army Field Support Center (AFSC) beginning in May 2009, including speeches, directives, or internal comms.
	•	Any documentation of his death from pancreatic cancer in 2010, honors received, or correspondence related to his funeral and burial at Arlington National Cemetery on September 9, 2010.
	4.	Legacy Sites and Honors
	•	Records regarding the 2011 naming of the DIA Contingency Operating Base in Kabul as Camp K.B. Reid, including naming justification, interagency approvals, and dedication ceremony photos or memos.
	•	Materials related to the Reid Center (DIA’s training facility in Norfolk, VA, established in 2012 and now closed).
	•	The establishment of the COL Kurush Bharucha-Reid Award for Excellence in HUMINT Tradecraft in 2014 by the HUMINT Training – Joint Center of Excellence, including award criteria, selection board minutes, and awardee names.
	•	Any correspondence, ceremony documentation, or Hall of Fame entry details from his 2014 induction into the Military Intelligence Corps Hall of Fame ￼.
	5.	Photographic and Ceremonial Records
	•	All official photographs, eulogies, obituaries, citations, press releases, internal honors, or other agency acknowledgments related to Col. Bharucha-Reid, both during service and posthumously.
	6.	Classified and Segregable Records
	•	If any records are classified, please initiate a mandatory declassification review (MDR) under Executive Order 13526 and release all segregable non-exempt portions under FOIA guidelines.
	7.	Delivery and Fee Waiver
	•	Please provide documents in searchable PDF format via secure email or download link.

This request supports a public-interest historical and educational project—specifically, a feature film titled Undercover Patriot exploring the intersection of identity, secrecy, and service. 

As a fellow Parsi Zoroastrian American, I believe Col. Bharucha-Reid’s legacy deserves recognition beyond the classified corridors in which he served.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Adil Minocherhomjee

title: 2022-10-24_Welfare Check FOIA Request

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: 2022-10-24_Welfare Check FOIA Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

All records relating to a welfare check (including any suicide welfare check) occurring on or about October 24, 2022, at 680 State Hwy 220, McDonough, New York, 13801 , involving Jonathan Rees. Incident reports; complaint reports; aided reports; case reports; narrative reports; call-for-service records; CAD records; dispatch logs; event chronologies; dispatcher notes; unit histories; 911 call recordings; non-emergency call recordings; audio recordings; transcripts; transcript excerpts; call summaries; records reflecting the reporting party’s self-identification; stated name of the reporting party; stated relationship of the reporting party to the subject; callback number; ANI (Automatic Number Identification); caller ID; telephone number associated with the caller; records showing the date and time of the call; records showing the nature of the complaint; records showing the disposition of the call; records identifying responding personnel.

If any portion of these records is exempt from disclosure, I request that all reasonably segregable non-exempt portions be provided. For any material withheld or redacted, please provide the specific statutory basis for each denial or redaction.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Pete Odin

communications.subject: City of Seattle Public Records Request Center - Invoice Ready :: P235492-081025
communications.communication:
This email is in further response to your public disclosure records request P235492-081025. The Seattle Police Department has records ready to be released pending payment. 
 
You have 30 days from today's date to pay for your records. If the City does not receive your payment within this time period, your request will be considered abandoned, and you will need to submit a new request. 
 SPD strongly encourages online payments. 
 
To pay online, please follow these steps: 
 
  * Navigate to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login. 
  * Find this request: P235492-081025. 
  * Select the 'View Invoices' button. 
 
  * Opens a pop-up window showing your invoice: INV26-P235492-1. 
  * Click on the invoice and then the 'Pay Now' button. 
  * Continue processing your payment. 
  * Select 'Done'. 
  * Once the payment process is done, you are returned to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * After your payment is successful you will receive an email from our payments center. After we receive your payment through the Police Records Request Center Secure Payments, the Public Disclosure Unit will release your records within one to two business days and send you a notice with instructions on how to download the records. 
 Please add seattle@mycusthelp.net to your E-mail contacts/address book to ensure delivery of the record center E-mails to your Inbox. (mailto:seattle@mycusthelp.net) 
 
This is an auto-generated email and has originated from an unmonitored email account. Please DO NOT REPLY.

04/08/2026 
 
Rose Terse 
191018-34140888@requests.muckrock.com 
 
RE: Public Disclosure Request#: P235492-081025 
 
Dear Rose Terse, 
 Dear Rose Terse, Your records have been released and are available on the Public Records Request Center to download. The records responsive to your request are two logs of PDRs with messages that were reassigned. This is the closest that we were able to obtain to what was requested. A message is required if the required completion date/ next installment date is changed, not if the request is assigned to a different staff member within the same unit. To download your records, please: 
 
  *  Navigate to the Public Records Request Center.
  *  Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  *  Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login. 
  *  Select this request: P235492-081025. 
  *  Select the 'View Files' button. 
  *  You can download records individually or all at the same time by clicking 'Download All'. 
  *  Please save your records as you may only download them from the system three times. If you do not download your records within 30 days of request closure, the link to your records will expire. If you believe you have received privileged or confidential records in error, please notify the City of Seattle immediately, through the Public Records Request Center or by email. Privileged or confidential records provided or received in error should not be reviewed, used, disseminated, distributed, or copied, and should be destroyed immediately. Thank you in advance for your cooperation. SPD will take no further action on this request. If you have questions regarding the processing of this request or would like to initiate an administrative appeal regarding the response to your request, please do so within 10 business days. You may also seek judicial review of our response to your request for a period of one year, beginning as of the date of this message, consistent with RCW 42.56.550. Please include your name and address, a copy of any redacted files, and a copy of this letter together with a brief statement identifying the basis of the appeal. Please submit your appeal via GovQA, the mail, or deliver your appeal to: Seattle Police Department Attn: Legal Section 610 Fifth Avenue P.O. Box 34986 Seattle, WA 98124-4986 
 
If you have any questions, you may send a message using the Police Records Request Center or call the Public Disclosure Desk at 206-684-5481. 
 
Sincerely, 
Jamie von Kehler 
Seattle Police Department 
Legal Unit

title: Grant County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026

communications.subject: West Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Grant County, WV Opioid Settlement Funds Spring 2026
communications.communication:
Drew Solt 
1511 University Avenue, P.O. Box 6010
Morgantown, WV 26506-6010
March 24  2026

5 Highland Avenue
Petersburg, WV 26847

To whom it may concern,  

Under the West Virginia Freedom of Information Act, §29-B-1-1 et seq., I am requesting an opportunity to inspect or obtain copies of public records that pertain to County Name opioid settlement funds. Specifically, I am requesting: 
- Documentation of the amount of funds the county has received from the federal opioid lawsuit settlements from March 1, 2025 through today
- The application forms that any organization would submit to receive settlement funds
- Documentation of how much county-level funding has been awarded from March 1, 2025 through today
- Any completed application of an organization that has received settlement funds from the county from from March 1, 2025 through today
- Documentation of the process by which funds are awarded
- Any documents that contain the names of the individuals who review the funding applications and determine the awards
- Any documents that account for future plans from the county for the use of opioid settlement funds
If there are any fees for searching or copying these records, please inform me if the cost will exceed $25. However, I would also like to request a waiver of all fees in that the disclosure of the requested information is in the public interest and will contribute significantly to the public’s understanding of how the opioid settlement funds are being spent. I am a student journalist whose request is strictly for news-gathering purposes. This information is not being sought for commercial purposes. 
The West Virginia Freedom of Information Act requires a response to this request be made within five business days. If access to the records I am requesting will take longer than this amount of time, please contact me with information about when I might expect copies or the ability to inspect the requested records. 


If you deny any or all of this request, please cite each specific exemption you feel justifies the refusal to release the information and notify me of the appeal procedures available to me under the law. 
Thank you for considering my request. 
Sincerely, Drew Solt
drewsolt@yahoo.com
740-243-6793

title: Visitor List for GSA in 2025

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Visitor List for GSA in 2025
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Over the past few months, there have been multiple media reports of DOGE staff working in an enclave within GSA offices. This has included staff hired from the Executive Office of the President (EOP), OPM and the Department of Labor, among others. It has even been reported that some DOGE leadership continued to show up for meetings even after departing from government service.

I run a public-interest site that tracks information about DOGE's activities in government in the spirit of transparency. I would like to request visitor logs for this year at the GSA. If this is too extensive a request, I can provide a list of specific visitor names to look for. We can also constrain the request to be from January to the end of June, but I would prefer to instead provide a list of names. Thank you.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jacob Harris

communications.subject: [External Message Added] City of Berkeley public records request #26-647
communications.communication:
City of Berkeley ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-647: This is in response to your March 3, 2026 Public Records Act request No. 26-647. City staff have identified records responsive to your request. Staff anticipate being able to provide all responsive records no later than Tuesday, April 7th at 5 p.m.   The Mayor’s Form 700s are filed with and maintained by the state. (Gov. Code § 87200.) They are publicly available through the California Fair Political Practices Commission and can be viewed online via the following link: https://www.fppc.ca.gov/search-filings/form-700-search/search-filed-form-700s/ If you have any questions concerning your request, please feel free to contact me at kperez@berkeleyca.gov. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Berkeley.

Technical support: See our help page

communications.subject: [External Message Added] City of Berkeley public records request #26-647
communications.communication:
City of Berkeley ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-647: This is in response to your March 3, 2026 Public Records Act request No. 26-647. Your request is granted. The City has completed its search for responsive documents and as a courtesy has attached them to this letter. Additionally, portions of these documents have been redacted to protect personal information. (Cal. Gov. Code §§ 7922.000 (formerly Cal. Gov. Code §§ 6255.)) Certain documents and portions of documents have been withheld based upon the deliberative process and attorney-client privileges, and the privilege for preliminary notes and drafts. (Cal. Gov. Code §§ 7922.000 & 7927.705 & 7927.500). Please note that the Public Records Act defines “public records” to include records pertaining to City business which are “prepared, owned, used, or retained” by the City. (Cal. Gov. Code § 7920.530.) The Act therefore does not require the City to search for, collect or disclose personal records predating an official assuming office with the City. The Mayor’s Form 700s are filed with and maintained by the state. (Gov. Code § 87200.) They are publicly available through the California Fair Political Practices Commission and can be viewed online via the following link: https://www.fppc.ca.gov/search-filings/form-700-search/search-filed-form-700s/ With this response, staff have completed their work to process and respond to your Public Records Act request. If you have any questions concerning your request, please feel free to contact me at kperez@berkeleyca.gov.   ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City of Berkeley.

Technical support: See our help page
